Cook times:


C1 - 34min / C2 - 36min.
129 bags of scrap for 11: am
Weight checks - 11:00am.
Viscosity, WA, Brix
Nathan Topham Please be advised that the caramel currently appears somewhat runny; water activity testing will be performed, and the results will be sent.
I'm stopping the line due to a FM found in the product by Q.C at the MD. Justin, Nathan
The production line has just been stopped because a piece of metal was found in a finished product rejected by the metal detector. An investigation is currently underway.
We have called maintenance and we are going to look at it together to  investigate further
Scrap pouch 215 dury mm4 mm5 fixing 11 to 12
12 to 1:00 pm 120 scrap pouch

Charity Young Mary Montilla Maintenance inspected the rejected part and stated that the issue likely originates from the cereal; is it acceptable to remove the cereal tote currently in use and place it on hold while continuing the line, or do you want the material currently on the bed removed?

As of right now our plan is to scrap all of tunnel 1 we will also inspect the bedding from CAR depositor to inclusion hopper to ensure we have no FM.  Once inspection is done we have the team then empty tunnel 1.  In the mean time we will run the FM through all the x-rays in the MM areas MM3-5.  Maintenance team will inspect all scrappers rollers and bearings to verify for no FM is coming from the line.  Hervin is going to get with L2 maintenance team to verify if there is any FM coming from grinding machine.  Justin, Curt, Charity, and Katya.
Line is back up and running
Weight checks - 2:00pm.

Everyone 
After talking with my team, the line stop is a failure on my part. 
2 months ago in middle of the night I gave my team a foreign material decision matrix. And in it for metal FM I had wrote “stop line and investigate all category 1”. 
Huge oversight and I will get the updated to reflect- 

1st instance- line runs while investigating source of contamination. 
2nd instance- elevate monitoring to high alert. If raw is coming from L2 (like grahams) then contact them to investigate activities and equipment at time of ingredient prep, but line continues to run.
3rd confirmed instance is line stops and film rca initiated.
Current cook time 34 and 35 minutes
Enrober SoS
Depositor SoS

▓ 
The melting tank area garbage container is missing at the beginning of shift 2.
We are noticing that the caramel in the hopper looks more fluid and spreads more when a drop is extracted; it adheres to the inclusion or pre-piece, and its final appearance is thinner.
I will inform the supervisor and the line leader to see what adjustments can be made.
Labol si corresponde a la candidad dicha!!

Miriah Manzanares Alan Todd Charity Young Justin Garner grinder was inspected, and blades were removed to be better checked, but all of the are in good condition. So there is not risk of the metal coming from the grinding process.
What is our policy for stopping a line? Charity Young Kathleen Gilbert. I thought it was 3 hits within an 8 hour window

I see the setting is currently at 700. I recall it being 900 in the past, but I haven't seen or heard any information on when or why it was changed. Can we raise the rejection setting again? Dakota Adair Curt Willis Caleb York Jon Jensen Charity Young
During my MD check, I had 4 false rejections (over rejections). Something to consider further. Angelica Perez Charity Young
Caramel cook times
Depositor EoS
Enrober EoS
MM3 Yamato and Matrix Centerline and the arms are intact
MM4 Yamato and Matrix Centerline and the arms are intact
MM5 Yamato and Matrix Centerline and the arms are intact
Enrober tempering chart.
Enrober CL and shadowboards.
The pallet corresponds to the quantity of labels.
Caramel and bed CL.

You have to set the "tunnel 1 belt speed" to 1.2 (as per centerline) to be able to get 47% in the "belt thickness HMI" while having the "sprinkler roller" at 9.0% (as per centerline). If the "tunnel 1 belt speed" is set to 1.0, you will get 39% in the "belt thickness HMI" even if the "sprinkler roller" is set to 9.0%. William, Justin, Nathan.
Final shift: -204 Alan Todd Brandon Lance Nidia Lopez Corea Sofia Carcamo
One box was inspected, and no opened, doubles or empty pieces were observed.
A box was inspected and several pieces were observed together.
One box was inspected, and no opened, doubles or empty pieces were observed.
Mid-shift Scrap
Mid shift: -493. Alan ToddNidia Lopez CoreaSofia Carcamo
Final gusset empty
End of shift.
End Shift -1,231 Alan ToddNidia Lopez CoreaSofia Carcamo
Good morning, team! First-shift update: batch verified.
The pocket consists of 18 pieces and meets the specifications.

Good morning today when checking that the bands were fine it was noticed that tunnel 3 began to fall salt the line stopped and we checked where it came from, we realized that the rollers were covered with salt and we proceeded to check the tunnel and it also has a lot of salt
We are getting discolored chocolate coming out of the mini tempering unit. We are taking the pipes out and going to clean them. Curt Willis
We have employees already cleaning the tunnel and rollers.

During the inspection, of temper unit residue was detected inside the pipe (see highlighted area), accompanied by an unpleasant odor. It is necessary to schedule a cleaning and sanitization of the component to restore proper operating conditions.Charity Young Justin Garner, Miriah Manzanares
This is from the oil cleaning.  We than purge chocolate though until we get clean chocolate
It smells pretty bad
Under the plate

A lot of salt was found in tunnel 3. 


Tunnel was cleaned and QA is about to inspect it Justin Garner
The tunnel still has salt
Katya Farnsworth Justin Garner do you have a rough est. of how long 2000 lbs will run?
Someone has factory reset the check weigher for the pack off. Myself, Victor, and Jason are here trying to program...
waiting for the decorator temper
What about Enrober? Do we have temper?
Check weigher is fixed as far as I can get it. Correct net weights are set. Tare. And reject is good.
Line is running
Both belts C8 and C11 are not working. C11 wasn't put together correctly and C8 the belt is frayed. We will be accumulating product and putting it directly in the MM's Justin Garner

Victor Romero Sr. Katya Farnsworth

Since lugs are being stacked - these are fully direct product contact. 

Please ensure lugs are not placed directly on floor. 

Change gloves frequently between processes.
Reject belt after Xray is not working.  Maintenance is fixing it right now

The silver line on this pouch is permitted, measures less than 3mm, and is not on the front of the pouch.

Angelica Perez, Ebrima Dibba
I am sorry. I don't understand what you are talking about.
All these parts were left here and not assembled. These go to belts C12-C18. Please make sure to reassemble everything Elizabeth Hernandez Dakota Adair
Those parts had been there for days; Conner Boyd was notified yesterday and took photos. We installed some parts yesterday, but there were no screws for those specific ones.
Box printing is working. Had to do trial and error on line speed and timing delay to get the print where we needed it.
I have increased the temp of the buffer tank for decorator to 100 degrees. Chocolate coming out at 90 is a bit cold Justin Garner
The printer on the east is not in a high traffic area and shouldn't be touched
I have marked with tape where the printer on the west side should be positioned. If there are printer issues, please check this first.
At the time of performing the checks we realize that the date is wrongly put in the boxes because on one side the correct time comes out and on the other side a different time comes out

Having issues with the west side print head. It is not printing the correct time. Troy is working on it. We have stopped sending cases through the printer. It is only 1 side. The other one is fine.Justin Garner
We are currently by-passing printheads and stacking the pallet without the print, so it can be reworked later
Curt Willis, Candon Watt, do we have an update on special chocolate lot 126131-01 10227 for S2 Tates run?
7 pm tonight.  It can be here but we can’t load until it is released
The CW keeps stopping, causing the pouches to pile up; I’ve already notified Troy Baxter
Troy made adjustments to the CW, and it is now working correctly.
Matrix 6 is down; the product isn't dropping into the pouches. Troy is working on it. We have eight containers of accumulated product.
We don't have Temper in the decorator; we're going to stop the line.
Please try to get it back in temper
To provide some additional info, because it had been set back to factory settings, it was stopping when 2 pouches were too close together. I adjusted the settings to reject instead of shut down
Put back down temperature to 90F, previous adjustment  caused the temper to spike
Elizabeth Hernandez Dakota Adair we adjust water and mass temperatures while temperature on the buffer tank goes down, once temperature gets better there, put back mini temper unit at 28 and 30C
Box printing is working, but I understand we will run out of boxes within the next 30 ish minutes.

I wanted to provide a summary of the issue we had with printing.  Apologies for the length.
 
The timestamp on one side was correct, but completely different on the other side.  After some investigation on settings, I opted to call VideoJet support.  They informed me that our software version is roughly 15 versions behind the current recommended version (our technician failed to perform the upgrade when he was here 6-8 weeks ago).  Our version has a known bug that doesn't sync the system clocks properly and accounted for the different timestamps.  There is no way to upgrade the software without a technician coming onsite.
 
My solution was to take the idle "Leader" printer from the S3 jar area and operate the 2 Leader printers as independent machines, instead of having them link together.  This is what is happening now and printing is working.
I will work with VideoJet to schedule a technician to come onsite and upgrade our 2 sets of box printers to the latest version

Katya Farnsworth
Can you explain to cold?  This has really affected tempering.  I’m not understanding why it was raised

During the pause for the pack area, decorator chocolate got cold (-0.6). Operator was trying to warm it up, but it wasn't working.  When I looked at the buffer tank and piping, it wad barely warm. That was due to water not properly recirculating.  I had Chris G. help me with that. In the meantime I turned it up to 100 degrees to get back into temper range
We've run out of boxes; we're going to start using the WIP boxes.

Just for clarification to everyone.  The reason we have a positive temper target is because we were having a hard time getting into on the mini temper unit.

Since moving to 90 degrees in the buffer tank we have not had this issue.  We can run at the lower temper range it will not cause any issues
Ok.  Thank you for the clarification.  Next time if we need to do something like this let’s only go up 5 degrees instead of 10 please 🙏
Curt, are you still wanting the outlet pipe cooling jacket bypass hose on?
Let’s wait and see if that temperature drops with the chocolate we are adding
Fresh chocolate and the heater being off, until it hits setpoint, should help bring it down decently quick
Matrix 6 is down again; product isn't dropping into the pouches. Maintenance is here.
We already have about 500 scrap pouches—mostly MM6—including many high- and low-weight ones.
As discussed, I am sending the final label for sollich 2 for your approval. Angelica Perez Charity Young

I saw on the mini temper unit that everything that was going though was going straight to the decorator. 

I opened the valve to have it flow into is send helping temper better
Good catch
Doing this dropped it from 28.8 to 28.5 and decreasing

Before

After

The scraper for C8 is out of position, so we’re seeing higher build up on the belt. 

We’ve called maintenance and will be adjusting it to contact the belt now.
Attached is the final label for Sollich 2 for your approval. The lot number has been assigned based on the most recent information provided by Laura. Angelica Perez, Charity Young
Please do not include the time.
The entire run is one single lot number it has to be the exact same lot number for the entire production run.
Curt Willis with the change I made we are at a 2.0 temper
1.0 for slope and 2.0 for temper index
I called Charity Young, we have the rest of the cookies on hand in quarantine status. We can’t load the label to the line. Also what is it on this status Candon Watt, Miriah Manzanares
Conner is getting those over now

The LPNs we need taken off quarantine:
R230547
R230555
R230569
Charity Young
This is all one lot number. This should not include the shift or the time. Candon Watt

All three of these have the same Exp. Date: 27JUL26

However, two pallets (R230552 & R230575) had the same Exp. Date but were allowed to scan in.
I’ll also send over the LPs of the pallets that are in the warehouse to see if any of these are in quarantine.
are these all used?
Does this label look correct to you ?
Yes no need for the shift or time. I’d like to see the direction by Laura if possible.
These two are the only ones we’ve used, and I believe our line lead said they scanned in. I’ll confirm that in one moment.
These 3 are staged on S2 right now.
Thanks!
the 16926 in the beginning of the LOT number on this label looks correct, I will have Angelica Perez verify the other information

All of these are still in warehouse:

R230554
R230572

I’m working on releasing. Please send me the lot
Any chance we could use all of one lot before moving to a new lot? These are also in OK status.
Conner Boyd Dakota Adair how much more cookie do you need?  how much longer will you be running?
Status changed to OK
Everything else is correct: the item number, the Best by, and the product description. Also the amount
Thank you Angelica Perez
I spoke with Elizabeth about tonight's cleaning and we believe we won't be cleaning on our shift Charity Young
Thank you. We will proceed with labeling the pallets.

As of right now we have 20 pallets or 12,000 pouches (30869). 

24,000 pouches remaining.
Charity Young

Thank you Charity, I verified with my line lead and those pallets are scanning in. 

We’ll resume on the line once maintenance has adjusted C11 and C8.
Dakota Adair Conner Boyd S2/M678 Please document the Lv1 clean in book & use alcohol sanitizer after QA completes the inspection. QA is being asked to complete ATP swabs for this as well.
Thank you for all the hard work everyone
X-ray just rejected a bag containing a washer!! I've already spoken to the supervisor, and we're trying to figure out where it came from.

We had FM hit at 19:20 (Washer) on the X-ray. 

During this time the line was already down, while maintenance aligned C8, properly installed the scraper, and so QA could release the pallets staged to our line. 

The product we were producing was from the accumulation above the MMs. We have since investigated all accumulation bins and the surrounding area. Everything looks to be in place and no other FM detected. 

We’re investigating where the washer could’ve come from, so far we have not found a bolt missing a washer in any of the areas that could lead into the product. 
Dakota Adair Charity Young
Do you want us to put some time on hold? Charity Young
When was the last good check?
Actions: document timeline and all equipment verification, etc.
MD?
All checks are on time?
Yes Los X Ray a tiempo todos
If they are on time

I’ve spoke with Charity Young and we’re good to continue running since the line was down when this bag was found and we inspected the area before sending more product to the line. 

The Pouch was reported and found in X-Ray reject bin at 19:22

The time on the pouch was 19:14, so I believe we found it in a tight enough window. Especially since we were all only running a few bins of accumulation.
No check has failed
👍 No holds at this time. Three rejects in an 8 hour period requires a hold.
Conner Boyd we need to identify when the last time a loose washer would have been brought to the line.  Any work done that included washers? Or any loose washers in toolboxes etc?
Thank you for your detailed information.
It's not from us.
Curt Willis Chad Stone, Justin Garner. This is the second hit. Charity Young
Any ideas where this could have come from?  Where in the process do we have washers that could be coming loose? Caleb York
I don’t believe anything came loose, it could have been from the clean that we did and it could have been left on the line

Kathleen Gilbert

This is the 1st hit.

The one earlier was on S3
Thanks for the clarification
If we have three in the 8 hour period we will need to complete a line clearance and place all previous product on hold in the previous 8 hours
That's correct

R230548 is still on Quarantine. It’s the same date as the pallets we had on the line earlier, this one was just brought from warehouse.

Can we have its status changed please? Charity Young
These LPNs that EXP 07/27/26 must be used before the ones that EXP 07/28/26
We have to go in FIFO - in this case by EXP date - we don't want to go back & forth on the finished EXP date

Yes, we are going off FIFO. 

The pallet with LP#:R230548 has the EXP 07/27/2026
From what I’ve seen of the cookies pulled down today, there isn’t a pallet with 7/28/2026 here.

Elizabeth and Dakota, I recommend stopping SL2 so that we can correct C11 belt. The belt stops due to the tracking timeout, caused by it being reassembled improperly. The belt should not drag on the photo-eyes. Do we know if the roller was removed when C11 was cleaned?

As of right now we have 42 pallets, or 25,200/36,000: 
10,800 pouches remaining
Joseph Scott you’ll just need to change the shift number on printers in MM area when you start at 11PM.
It's just the last digit in the first text block. Change 2 to 3
Conner Boyd Troy Baxter why is the shift on this?  I don't see that on the spec. am I missing it?  I see the Line & the M# but not a shift.
It’s just setup to have the shift # on the box printers, not the pouches.
Ok.
Shift 3

57 pallets and 94 we run all the pouches we had 
 
We were short 2 pallets and 6 boxes 
 
Curt Willis
Chocolate changeover done
The hapman for salt was not ready, the operator will install it.  Justin Garner
We have already received the boxes and we are starting the line.

We are getting a Russel finex alarm on the ernrober. Maintenance is going to check the pin and see if it’s broke. 

I will send an update if we need to pause and clean the filter and replace the pin.
First shift centerlines
We are back depositing
Good morning, team! Batch verification complete. The weight is within specifications, the product appearance is acceptable, and the unit passed the water leak test.
WDS Centerlines
Finished WIP doing level 2 clean and set up for mint 16oz-pallet240
Startup checklist
The line started at time
Set at 18 mpm
we are having issues with the bags, the check weight is reinject the bags with 31 pieces (the target is 32), maintenance is on the line
Great temper control!
Metal Detector belt and C6 belt are correctly aligned

The estimated time to complete the order and finish the changeover is approximately between 9:30 and 10:00 a.m. Maintenance will be notified once we are finished so they can begin the changeover. Preston Elmer
While checking the flow-wrap packaging, I found 9 open units out of a box of 1,050—an average open-unit rate of 0.8%.
We have completed the 12g order and are now starting the changeover to 35g.
Change over was started and most of the crew was sent to lunch and to rotate out MFM
Order completed
Starting taking Temper
The CLs do not match the settings on the Temper Unit HMI. Do you want us to adjust them to the values indicated by the CLs? Robert Villarreal Preston Elmer Shawn Jewkes
We are loading molds at a speed of 10 MPM, with 2 employees inspecting molds in the shaker area. The nozzle plate has been changed, and Maintenance continues working on the Cavanna changeover.

Really good question. Like Preston indicated, we should match the last setpoints from the 12 gram. We made the changes to the water jacket system since this recipe was last run, so we expect to see setpoints differ from the original CL.
This why we want to start now so Preston E can come take a look if needed
It's already tempered with the old 35g settings, so it may take some time to bring it back to nominal.
Do you want the operator to make the adjustments and set the plain recipe to the same values we used this morning for 12g, or would you prefer to come make the changes yourself? Preston Elmer
Go ahead and make them, going up from low temper is easier than going down from high temper.
Yes please
I would start at the last settings that we had on 12 gram and go from there
I matched what we had on 12g to these CL's. I wanted to avoid issues going back to the old settings
Cavanna line is now good to go
Bosch is good to go now
Maintenance makes adjustments to the mixing water at 12:29 AM.

This is rock stable at 81.6 when desired is 79.2. the PID for crystallization is tuned extremely slow. But since we are -0.5 at "81.6" then I think I should set it to 81.5 as a starting point. What do you think Robert Villarreal ?
Maintenance is making new adjustments to the mixing water and crystallization.
Chad Stone we have two good tempers we want to start the line
Aligned. Start
I still don't understand how its starting up in negative tempers but if its staying steady around there I'd be happy to set it at 81.5 as centerline.
QA always wants 3 good tempers first, I directed them to start the line with 2 good tempers and if the 3 comes out bad we will remelt the chocolate
Third temper came out at -0.1
The line has just started.
We are having issues with some sensors at Cavanna; maintenance is on the line.
The weight of the chocolate bar is within the range.
Please be on the look out for a roughly 2.5" x 2.5" plastic puck.
Everyone one of the risers on the metal detector feet is missing, the metal detector is only standing on 3 legs. Has anyone seen it's foot?? I can't imagine this would help with belt tracking.
Maintenance staff replaced the finger, but it continues to eject chocolate. Maintenance continues on the line.
The film we are using is printed with “Best By,” but we have a requirement we must meet, so we need to add the text “Best If Used By.”
Try to keep the text inside the box
maintenance is  still in the line working on the Dooner 3 belt ,because it won't start.
Cavanna  continues rejecting chocolate as it enters the rail

Flow-wrap packaging check update 


The flow wrap is well-sealed along the sides, and the "best  If Used by" date is legible and clearly printed in the correct location; the appearance is acceptable but could be improved, and the team leader has already been notified regarding these improvements.
ATP passed for nozzle place
Metal Detector belt and C6 belt are running correctly.
Glycol is on the smile of the smiling face
Start up checklist

Can we adjust the print off mark on the lot please? 

Let’s monitor this issue Lorena Abreu Nathan Gevenois
I’ll go look at it now
Do we want that to say Best By aaand Best If Used By on this version of wrap? Nathan Gevenois Angel Cabrera
Gotcha gotcha!
It has to have both until Feastables can update the art work.
Metal Detector belt and C6 belt are running correctly.

The Feastables Milk 35g chocolate product line is operating stably and within established standards, with no deviations or issues observed during the production run. Weights are within specifications, the organoleptic evaluation is satisfactory, and temperatures have remained controlled. The screen appears to be in good condition, showing no damage or foreign material. Additionally, the packaging process (flow-wrap with no air leaks) and palletizing are being carried out in accordance with specifications. Overall, the line's performance has been satisfactory.
Glycol is good
Metal Detector belt and C6 belt are running correctly.
All belts are working correctly

Update from third shift: Product meets all specifications. Bar weight and depth dimensions are at target values. Lot placement for film has been correctly. Packaging is following all specifications. Correct lot is printed on both the display/film. Silver part on the back of the film is below 3mm. The film packaging is centered. Production is using the correct shipping box label. No issues to report.
A master case was checked and we have 6% of the open bars already working with Raoul and the line lead to reduce that percentage
Chad Stone We changed it
We changed the mass pump speed to the 15 MPM
Changed MPS to 1757
Lot code after 12

Raoul Lubain, Alan Greer

We need to slow down to 15 MPM and have inspectors if we are seeing this. 

Preston Elmer, Chris Givens

Need your focus in the morning on this.  

We knew we had issues on 12g and thought it was other factors. 

We have not seen this issue on 35g in 2 years we could t fix.
Imperial Good morning, could you post the tempers from the last 2 hours please? Thanks!Raoul Lubain Alan Greer
Sure
Raoul Lubain can you have the operator do a temper test at the new speed please or post to chat if a test has already been done?
We are having some bars that are coming out with marks, the operator is already making adjustments to improve the product, the temperature of the molds and the chocolate is in the correct parameters

Update from third shift: Product meets all specifications. Bar weight and depth dimensions are at target values. Lot placement for film has been correctly. Packaging is following all specifications. Correct lot is printed on both the display/film. Silver part on the back of the film is below 3mm. The film packaging is centered. Production is using the correct shipping box label. 
A master case was checked and we have 2% of the open bars.

We should be making adjustments sooner to correct temper in the yellow. As the paper says at the bottom "If temper reaches the yellow zones, send a message on teams and make adjustments. Check temper every 15 minutes until temper returns to the "Ideal" zone."
I'm not seeing 15min adjustments and not seeing adjustments made when in yellow. First adjustment made at 345 this morning and I made one 10min ago.
I checked with the operator
Midshift
The change made by maintenance.

Raoul Lubain

Were there more checks between the 3:59am and 4:59 check?
I went big when I saw that depositing had stopped and there was no risk to product.
Chad Stone yes, it was good. 0.4

Raoul Lubain, Alan Greer

How are the % of open bars?

Are we capturing all of them that come off Cavanna?

Did we find any trends of open bars when splicing from E -> W or W -> E?
Chad Stone Most of them from the E side. Since we changed the MPM speed from 18  to 15 mpm it's getting better
1.1 after the 0.9. the full degree change likely hasn't taken fully. But I went down .5 more anyways to stay ahead of it.
Is it while switching or while continuously running Raoul Lubain?

Update from third shift: Product meets all specifications. Bar weight and depth dimensions are at target values. Lot placement for film has been correctly. Packaging is following all specifications. Correct lot is printed on both the display/film. Silver part on the back of the film is below 3mm. The film packaging is centered. Production is using the correct shipping box label. 
A master case was checked and we have 0% of the open bars.
We will follow the same troubleshooting process as on 12g to eliminate this today while ramping speed back up
Chad Stone, Before and after most of them from the the East side.
The hopper has been emptied Tyson is still working on it

Once I got to 82 on water and it still wasn't changing, I decided to hit crystalization and dropped it to 80.0 and we got into the negatives, -0.3, so bumping mixing back up .2 and we can run soon if it doesn't continue to drop.
Countdown
We have an acceptable temper; we are taking another Temper . The hoppers are at 120°; the operator has already lowered the temperature.
This should be a much better take off than the last few temper struggles.

The temper remains acceptable, but we cannot start yet because the hoppers have not reached the correct temperature. At 6:30 a.m., the hoppers were adjusted because they were at 120°F at the start of the shift. Once they reach the correct temperature, we will start the line.
Preston Elmer Grey Boscan Qa, Maintenance, and operator are inspecting the second mold jam
Chocolate is now being deposited into the hopper.

An inspection of the Imperial machine was conducted twice with the team leader and the maintenance department after the fault alarm triggered and stopped the machine because the sensor had detected an anomaly in the molds but it turned out to be only slightly dirty.
Air nozzles at twin belts were not at the correct measurements. Was causing chocolate to not get blown off properly and cause jams. Adjustment made. Now running good.

I noticed there was a random piece inside the Mold Return. I was told that it tends to fall out here and there. Production put it back to its original place (picture attached)

Everyone these doors are still getting dropped when opening and are now starting to buckle in the middle. This issue happened sometimes in L2 and we had to straighten them once in a while but this is so frequent now that the doors are showing more damage than L2 doors every showed, and a lot less run hours. I need your help to educate the teams on this and bring it to their attention, the doors need to be set down deliberately and not dropped to hit the bumpers hard. Tyson Fausett Kimberly Morales Itzel Chavez Rich Brierley Frederick Peairs Marcos Montano Alan Greer Rose Smuin Chad Stone
